note: System-assumption meta-node (authority-model Tier F): the truths the EVALUATORS rely on. A wrong assumption silently flips a control's state, so we model assumptions as first-class, monitored nodes. kind 'verifiable' has an executable check (holds/VIOLATED); kind 'accepted' is a known limitation/heuristic surfaced so it is never forgotten (not auto-checkable). affects_controls links an assumption to the controls it could corrupt ('*' = all Form-D-driven controls).

assumptions.statement: EDGAR Form D structured XML is at primary_doc.xml (strip the xsl-prefix from submissions' primaryDocument).
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Form D parse fails or yields the rendered HTML -> every control's state is silently wrong.

assumptions.statement: Form D field localnames are stable: entityName, federalExemptionsExclusions/item, hasNonAccreditedInvestors, dateOfFirstSale/value, relatedPersonInfo.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A field rename empties an evaluator input -> control silently flips to open/wrong.

assumptions.statement: Form D federalExemptionsExclusions encodes Rule 506(b) as the literal code '06b'.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: exemption_basis control mis-detects the offering type.

assumptions.statement: eCFR versioner /versions/title-17.json?part=NNN returns content_versions[] with identifier + amendment_date.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Codified-law drift detection silently stops working (every node reads unverifiable).

assumptions.statement: Federal Register API returns results[] with a cfr_references field ([{title, part}]).
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Release/order drift mapping silently breaks (binding-but-uncodified gap reopens).

assumptions.statement: SEC Administrative Proceedings RSS (admin.xml) returns <item><title> entries.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Bad-actor drift monitor silently returns no matches (looks clean when it is blind).

assumptions.statement: Bad-actor matching is case-insensitive substring matching; false positives are EXPECTED and resolved by human disambiguation.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Over-flags (handled: every hit is escalate-to-counsel, never a conclusion) or misses name variants (under-flags).

assumptions.statement: The AP RSS is a rolling window, not the full history; a non-match is NOT a clearance (historical SALI check pending).
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A non-match could be read as a clearance. Mitigated: output says 'NOT a clearance'.

assumptions.statement: The integration control uses a +/-180-day EDGAR window as a PUBLIC-SIDE proxy; it is not the legal facts-and-circumstances integration test.
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Window misses an integration concern outside 180 days. Mitigated: control never 'satisfied'; counsel owns integration.
